title: sphaeralcea remota
intro: Sphaeralcea remota, also known as Desert Marigold or Mexican Gold Poppy, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, derived from its alkaloids and saponins. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Sphaeralcea remota is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
